name: plan
description: Guidelines for iteratively planning tasks and changes before implementation. Use this when the user requests a non-trivial task or when you need to align on a complex implementation strategy.
capabilities: Guide AI to explore codebase before planning
Structure plans with objective, approach, steps, and verification
Generate clarifying questions with proposed answers
Wait for user approval before implementation
author: 0xmsc
securitySummary: This is a pure markdown documentation skill containing only behavioral guidelines. No executable code, network calls, filesystem access, or external command execution. The static scanner flagged false positives: backtick references were markdown tool names, 'DES' was 'Design Up Front', and metadata URLs were configuration. All 21 findings are FALSE_POSITIVEs.
valueStatement: Complex AI tasks often fail due to unclear scope or missing requirements. This skill provides a structured workflow for gathering requirements, drafting implementation plans, and getting user approval before writing any code.
limitations: Does not execute planning steps automatically
Does not integrate with project management tools
Does not generate code or modify files
Relies on user to provide approval

useCases.title: Feature Implementation Planning
useCases.description: Plan new features with clear scope, dependencies, and verification steps before coding begins.

useCases.title: Complex Task Decomposition
useCases.description: Break down large AI tasks into manageable milestones with iterative feedback loops.

useCases.title: Architecture Review Workflow
useCases.description: Establish consistent planning process for team code changes and architectural decisions.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Planning Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Plan the implementation of [describe your task]. Follow the planning workflow: gather information, draft a plan with objective, approach, steps, and verification, then ask clarifying questions.

promptTemplates.title: Refine Existing Plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Refine the previous plan based on this feedback: [describe changes]. Update the plan and wait for my approval before proceeding.

promptTemplates.title: Risk-Aware Planning
promptTemplates.prompt: Plan a [describe dangerous operation like bulk refactoring or database migration]. Identify the most risky parts and propose safe verification steps for each.

promptTemplates.title: Exploration-First Planning
promptTemplates.prompt: Explore the codebase to understand [specific area]. List dependencies and existing patterns, then draft a plan that aligns with the codebase conventions.

bestPractices: Always explore the codebase before planning to understand existing patterns
Include at least three clarifying questions with proposed answers
Wait for explicit user approval before starting implementation
antiPatterns: Starting implementation without user approval or clarification
Creating overly detailed plans for massive tasks
Skipping risk identification for dangerous operations

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It provides planning guidelines for any LLM coding assistant.

faq.question: How detailed should the plan be?
faq.answer: Keep plans concise and actionable. Focus on the immediate milestone rather than planning every detail upfront.

faq.question: Can I integrate with Jira or other tools?
faq.answer: This skill does not integrate with external tools. It provides verbal planning workflow between you and the AI.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill only contains documentation guidelines. It does not access files, network, or send data anywhere.

faq.question: What if the plan needs changes during implementation?
faq.answer: Stop implementation, explain the deviation, and ask for approval on the updated plan before continuing.

faq.question: How is this different from other planning approaches?
faq.answer: This skill enforces an iterative approach with explicit approval checkpoints rather than upfront comprehensive planning.
